Meaning.En: A curved piece of wood or other material used with a string to shoot arrows.
Meaning.Example: He practiced archery with a traditional longbow.

Situation.Vi: Trong môn bắn cung, người chơi cầm cung bằng tay trái và kéo dây bằng tay phải.
Situation.En: In archery, the archer holds the bow with the left hand and draws the string with the right.

Meaning.Vi: Nút thắt (trên dây đàn violin, guitar, v.v.)
Meaning.En: A knot or loop in a string of a musical instrument, especially a violin, used to secure the string to the peg or tailpiece.
Meaning.Example: The violinist adjusted the bow on the tailpiece before tuning.

Situation.Vi: Khi thay dây đàn, nghệ sĩ cần buộc lại nút thắt ở cuối dây đàn.
Situation.En: When changing strings, the musician needs to reattach the bow knot at the end of the string.

Meaning.Vi: Đoạn cong (trên thân đàn violin, dùng để kéo dây)
Meaning.En: The curved stick with horsehair stretched between its ends, used to play string instruments like the violin.
Meaning.Example: She rosined the bow before playing the concerto.

Situation.Vi: Nghệ sĩ vỗ nhẹ vào rosin trước khi kéo đàn bằng cần kéo.
Situation.En: The musician taps rosin onto the bow before playing the concerto with it.

Meaning.Vi: Sự cúi người, sự chào (để thể hiện kính trọng)
Meaning.En: A movement in which the head or upper body is bent forward, especially as a sign of respect or greeting.
Meaning.Example: The audience rose and bowed to the conductor after the performance.

Situation.Vi: Sau buổi biểu diễn, các nghệ sĩ cúi chào khán giả để cảm ơn.
Situation.En: After the performance, the performers bowed to the audience to express gratitude.

Meaning.Vi: Cúi người, chào (để thể hiện kính trọng)
Meaning.En: To bend the head or body forward, especially as a sign of respect, submission, or greeting.
Meaning.Example: She bowed deeply to the audience before beginning her speech.

Situation.Vi: Trong nghi lễ truyền thống, các học trò phải cúi chào thầy giáo trước khi vào lớp.
Situation.En: In traditional ceremonies, students must bow to their teacher before entering the classroom.

Meaning.Vi: Tuân theo, phục tùng (một cách khiêm tốn)
Meaning.En: To submit oneself humbly to someone else’s authority or wishes.
Meaning.Example: He bowed to the pressure and resigned from his position.

Situation.Vi: Sau nhiều lần phản đối, ông ấy cuối cùng cũng chấp nhận quy định mới.
Situation.En: After repeated objections, he finally bowed to the new regulation.

Meaning.En: To play a stringed instrument using a bow.
Meaning.Example: The violinist bowed the strings gently to produce a soft melody.

Situation.Vi: Nghệ sĩ bắt đầu bằng cách kéo nhẹ cần kéo trên dây đàn.
Situation.En: The musician began by gently bowing the strings to warm up.
